example : ¬ Integrable (fun _ ↦ 1 : ℝ → ℝ) := by
  intro h
  have h₁ : ¬(Integrable (fun _ : ℝ => (1 : ℝ))) := by
    intro h₂
    -- We use the fact that the integral of the constant function 1 over ℝ is not finite.
    have h₃ : ∫ x : ℝ, (1 : ℝ) = ∫ x : ℝ, (1 : ℝ) := rfl
    have h₄ : ∫ x : ℝ, (1 : ℝ) ≠ (0 : ℝ) := by
      -- The integral of 1 over ℝ is not zero.
      have h₅ : (∫ x : ℝ, (1 : ℝ)) = ENNReal.ofReal (volume.univ : ℝ≥0∞) := by
        simp [intervalIntegral.integral_const, Real.volume_Ioi]
        <;> norm_num
        <;> simp_all [MeasureTheory.integral_const]
        <;> field_simp
        <;> ring_nf
        <;> norm_num
        <;> simp_all [MeasureTheory.integral_const]
        <;> linarith
      have h₆ : volume.univ = ∞ := by
        -- The measure of the entire real line is infinity.
        simp [Real.volume_Ioi, ENNReal.ofReal_eq_top]
        <;> norm_num
        <;> simp_all [MeasureTheory.integral_const]
        <;> linarith
      have h₇ : ENNReal.ofReal (volume.univ : ℝ≥0∞) = ∞ := by
        rw [h₆]
        <;> simp [ENNReal.ofReal_eq_top]
        <;> norm_num
        <;> simp_all [MeasureTheory.integral_const]
        <;> linarith
      have h₈ : (∫ x : ℝ, (1 : ℝ)) = ∞ := by
        rw [h₅]
        <;> rw [h₇]
        <;> simp [ENNReal.ofReal_eq_top]
        <;> norm_num
        <;> simp_all [MeasureTheory.integral_const]
        <;> linarith
      have h₉ : (∫ x : ℝ, (1 : ℝ)) ≠ (0 : ℝ) := by
        intro h₁₀
        rw [h₈] at h₁₀
        norm_num at h₁₀ ⊢
        <;> simp_all [ENNReal.ofReal_eq_top]
        <;> linarith
      exact h₉
    -- But if the function were integrable, its integral would be zero.
    have h₅ : (∫ x : ℝ, (1 : ℝ)) = 0 := by
      have h₆ : Integrable (fun _ : ℝ => (1 : ℝ)) := h₂
      simp [h₆.integral_eq_zero_of_ae_stronglyMeasurable]
      <;>
      (try norm_num) <;>
      (try simp_all [MeasureTheory.integral_const]) <;>
      (try linarith) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try
        {
          have h₇ : ∫ x : ℝ, (1 : ℝ) = ENNReal.ofReal (volume.univ : ℝ≥0∞) := by
            simp [intervalIntegral.integral_const, Real.volume_Ioi]
            <;> norm_num
            <;> simp_all [MeasureTheory.integral_const]
            <;> field_simp
            <;> ring_nf
            <;> norm_num
            <;> simp_all [MeasureTheory.integral_const]
            <;> linarith
          have h₈ : volume.univ = ∞ := by
            simp [Real.volume_Ioi, ENNReal.ofReal_eq_top]
            <;> norm_num
            <;> simp_all [MeasureTheory.integral_const]
            <;> linarith
          have h₉ : ENNReal.ofReal (volume.univ : ℝ≥0∞) = ∞ := by
            rw [h₈]
            <;> simp [ENNReal.ofReal_eq_top]
            <;> norm_num
            <;> simp_all [MeasureTheory.integral_const]
            <;> linarith
          have h₁₀ : (∫ x : ℝ, (1 : ℝ)) = ∞ := by
            rw [h₇]
            <;> rw [h₉]
            <;> simp [ENNReal.ofReal_eq_top]
            <;> norm_num
            <;> simp_all [MeasureTheory.integral_const]
            <;> linarith
          simp_all [ENNReal.ofReal_eq_top]
          <;> norm_num at *
          <;> simp_all [MeasureTheory.integral_const]
          <;> linarith
        }) <;>
      (try

          simp_all [MeasureTheory.integral_const]
          <;> norm_num at *
          <;> linarith

      have h₈ : (∫ x : ℝ, (1 : ℝ)) = 0 := h₅
      exact h₇ h₈
    exact h₆

      simpa [integral_const, Real.volume_Ioi] using h₄
  -- The integral cannot be both finite and ∞, so we have a contradiction.
  have h₃ : ¬((∫ x : ℝ, (1 : ℝ) ∂volume : ℝ) < ∞) := by
    simpa [h₁] using not_lt_top _
  exact h₃ h₂

have h₁ : ¬ Integrable (fun _ ↦ 1 : ℝ → ℝ) := by
    intro h
    have h₂ : Integrable (fun _ ↦ (1 : ℝ)) := h
    have h₃ : ∫ (x : ℝ), (1 : ℝ) ∂volume = ∞ := by
      -- The integral of 1 over ℝ is infinity
      simp [integral_const, volume_ne_zero]
      <;>
      simp_all [Real.volume_Icc]
      <;>
      norm_num
      <;>
      simp_all [ENNReal.ofReal_eq_zero]
      <;>
      linarith
    have h₄ : ∫ (x : ℝ), (1 : ℝ) ∂volume < ∞ := by
      -- The integral of an integrable function is finite
      have h₅ : ∫ (x : ℝ), (1 : ℝ) ∂volume < ∞ := by
        -- Use the fact that the integral of an integrable function is finite
        have h₆ : Integrable (fun _ ↦ (1 : ℝ)) := h₂
        have h₇ : ∫ (x : ℝ), (1 : ℝ) ∂volume < ∞ := by
          -- The integral of an integrable function is finite
          have h₈ : ∫ (x : ℝ), (1 : ℝ) ∂volume = ∫ (x : ℝ), (1 : ℝ) ∂volume := rfl
          rw [h₈]
          exact by
            -- Use the fact that the integral of an integrable function is finite
            have h₉ : ∫ (x : ℝ), (1 : ℝ) ∂volume < ∞ := by
              -- Use the fact that the integral of an integrable function is finite
              have h₁₀ : ∫ (x : ℝ), (1 : ℝ) ∂volume = ∫ (x : ℝ), (1 : ℝ) ∂volume := rfl
              rw [h₁₀]
              exact by
                -- Use the fact that the integral of an integrable function is finite
                have h₁₁ : ∫ (x : ℝ), (1 : ℝ) ∂volume < ∞ := by
                  -- Use the fact that the integral of an integrable function is finite
                  exact (by
                    -- Use the fact that the integral of an integrable function is finite
                    have h₁₂ : Integrable (fun _ ↦ (1 : ℝ)) := h₂
                    have h₁₃ : ∫ (x : ℝ), (1 : ℝ) ∂volume < ∞ := by
                      -- Use the fact that the integral of an integrable function is finite
                      exact (by
                        -- Use the fact that the integral of an integrable function is finite
                        simp_all [integral_const, volume_ne_zero]
                        <;>
                        simp_all [Real.volume_Icc]
                        <;>
                        norm_num
                        <;>
                        simp_all [ENNReal.ofReal_eq_zero]
                        <;>
                        linarith
                      )
                    exact h₁₃
                  )
                exact h₁₁
              <;>
              simp_all [integral_const, volume_ne_zero]
              <;>
              simp_all [Real.volume_Icc]
              <;>
              norm_num
              <;>
              simp_all [ENNReal.ofReal_eq_zero]
              <;>
              linarith
            exact h₉
          <;>
          simp_all [integral_const, volume_ne_zero]
          <;>
          simp_all [Real.volume_Icc]
          <;>
          norm_num
          <;>
          simp_all [ENNReal.ofReal_eq_zero]
          <;>
          linarith
        exact h₇
      exact h₅
    -- Contradiction: the integral cannot be both finite and infinite
    have h₅ : ¬(∫ (x : ℝ), (1 : ℝ) ∂volume < ∞) := by
      intro h₆
      have h₇ : ∫ (x : ℝ), (1 : ℝ) ∂volume = ∞ := h₃
      rw [h₇] at h₆
      norm_num at h₆
    exact h₅ h₄
  exact h₁

intro h
  have h₁ : (fun _ : ℝ => (1 : ℝ)) = (fun _ : ℝ => (1 : ℝ)) := rfl
  rw [h₁] at h
  -- Use the fact that the integral of 1 over ℝ is infinite to derive a contradiction
  have h₂ : ¬Integrable (fun _ : ℝ => (1 : ℝ)) := by
    intro h₃
    -- The integral of 1 over ℝ is infinite, which contradicts the integrability
    have h₄ : ∫ (x : ℝ), (1 : ℝ) = ∞ := by
      -- Compute the integral of 1 over ℝ
      have h₅ : ∫ (x : ℝ), (1 : ℝ) = ∞ := by
        -- The integral of 1 over ℝ is the same as the volume of ℝ, which is infinite
        simp [integral_const]
        <;>
        simp_all [volume_Iio]
        <;>
        norm_num
        <;>
        simp_all [ENNReal.top_eq]
        <;>
        aesop
      exact h₅
    -- The integral of 1 over ℝ is finite if 1 is integrable
    have h₅ : ∫ (x : ℝ), (1 : ℝ) ≠ ∞ := by
      -- Use the fact that 1 is integrable to show that the integral is finite
      have h₆ : Integrable (fun _ : ℝ => (1 : ℝ)) := h₃
      have h₇ : ∫ (x : ℝ), (1 : ℝ) ≠ ∞ := by
        -- Use the fact that the integral of an integrable function is finite
        simp_all [integral_eq_zero_of_not_integrable]
        <;>
        aesop
      exact h₇
    -- Contradiction arises as the integral cannot be both finite and infinite
    have h₆ : False := by
      -- Use the fact that the integral cannot be both finite and infinite
      simp_all
      <;>
      aesop
    -- Conclude the proof
    exact h₆
  -- Conclude the proof
  exact h₂ h
